en_body:
Orchard branches heavy with fruit
The leather globe swaying, weighted with seeds

 O my Beloved...
 Most beautiful beneath Heaven!
A pomegranate cleft in two
Rose-red seeds within the teeth
Your lips like sections of orange, tangerine
Your eyes deep-black, mystic longan seeds
weeping drops of Hồng-Hà, Hương-Giang, Cửu-Long
The time of tearing-apart has passed
May Peace arise and prosper through the Five Continents
A child's smile blossoms
Beside a bowl of Nàng-Hương rice,
Hair black-lustrous, blue-green
The Beloved's shoulder, white hand, white blouse
The city murmurs, stirring young men's hearts
And the scholar-brothers, the gracious sisters
Sing the Song-of-Rejoicing
The grand orchestra of the Rising-Song
 soaring beyond storm and tempest like a river in flood-season
the sound of hò, folk-song, vọng-cổ
 pounding mountain and rapid, beating rhythms against walls high and low
The Beloved climbs the low and high notes
As riding ocean-waves where Mother Sea caresses the fragrant shore
And returns to green fields of maiden rice-seedlings
The season of green cốm, blue-green across Time
He leans back within the slow-and-quickened dream
Drowsing, dreaming with the harvest hò khoan sowing Love!
(In the Collection "LuiKang, Words the Self-Immolation Evoked:
 April, 2006)

Portrait of the Beloved
Orchard branches heavy with fruit
The leather globe swaying, weighted with seeds

 O my Beloved...
 Most beautiful beneath Heaven!
A pomegranate cleft in two
Rose-red seeds within the teeth
Your lips like sections of orange, tangerine
Your eyes deep-black, mystic longan seeds
weeping drops of Hồng-Hà, Hương-Giang, Cửu-Long
The time of tearing-apart has passed
May Peace arise and prosper through the Five Continents
A child's smile blossoms
Beside a bowl of Nàng-Hương rice,
Hair black-lustrous, blue-green
The Beloved's shoulder, white hand, white blouse
The city murmurs, stirring young men's hearts
And the scholar-brothers, the gracious sisters
Sing the Song-of-Rejoicing
The grand orchestra of the Rising-Song
 soaring beyond storm and tempest like a river in flood-season
the sound of hò, folk-song, vọng-cổ
 pounding mountain and rapid, beating rhythms against walls high and low
The Beloved climbs the low and high notes
As riding ocean-waves where Mother Sea caresses the fragrant shore
And returns to green fields of maiden rice-seedlings
The season of green cốm, blue-green across Time
He leans back within the slow-and-quickened dream
Drowsing, dreaming with the harvest hò khoan sowing Love!
(In the Collection "LuiKang, Words the Self-Immolation Evoked:
 April, 2006)
note: Hồng-Hà, Hương-Giang, and Cửu-Long are Vietnam's Red, Perfume, and Mekong rivers; hò, vọng-cổ, and hò khoan are traditional Vietnamese song forms.
